source.title: Clinical Overview of Ringworm
source.population: People with suspected ringworm or dermatophyte infection, including skin, scalp, foot, groin, and nail presentations.
source.setting: Clinical recognition, exposure assessment, and diagnostic testing for suspected fungal skin or nail infection.
source.exclusions: Partial for selected tinea-corporis identity, exposure, lesion-feature, extent, and testing documentation. It does not support UAE pathways, autonomous diagnosis, medicine, dose, treatment, referral urgency, or fixed follow-up.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis
source.population: Infants, adolescents, and adults with suspected or clinician-diagnosed seborrheic dermatitis, with differential and referral qualifiers.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected seborrheic-dermatitis symptoms, distribution, appearance, red flags, differential testing, and documentation-only management fields. Diagnosis, medicine choice, dose, treatment instruction, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports ringworm, tinea, dermatophyte, circular scaly rash, body-site terminology, spread, person, animal, and contaminated-surface exposure context.

assessment.text: Supports erythematous or differently pigmented raised scaly rings, central clearing, multiple or extensive lesions, and exudative lesions when bacterial superinfection has developed.
assessment.rationale: Supports annular, scaly, raised-border, central-clearing, extent, widespread, and secondary-infection documentation without diagnosing tinea.
